questions.text: What does the title 'Fussilat' mean?
questions.answer: It means 'Explained in Detail', referring to the verses of the Quran being clearly explained for a people who know.

questions.text: What was the reaction of the disbelievers when the Quran was recited to them (Verse 5)?
questions.answer: They said their hearts were under coverings, their ears were deaf, and there was a screen between them and the Prophet.

questions.text: How many days did it take for Allah to create the earth and its provisions?
questions.answer: The earth was created in two days, and its provisions were ordained in four days (totaling four for the earth's process).

questions.text: What was the response of the heavens and earth when Allah commanded them to come into being?
questions.answer: They said, 'We come willingly'.

questions.text: How did Allah adorn the lowest heaven?
questions.answer: With lamps (stars) and as a protection.

questions.text: What was the punishment for the people of 'Ad?
questions.answer: A screaming, cold wind during days of misfortune.

questions.text: What was the pride of the people of 'Ad (Verse 15)?
questions.answer: They said, 'Who is greater than us in strength?'.

questions.text: What will testify against the enemies of Allah on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Their hearing, their sight, and their skins.

questions.text: What will the people ask their skins on that Day?
questions.answer: 'Why have you testified against us?' and the skins will reply that Allah, who gives speech to all things, gave them speech.

questions.text: What did the disbelievers suggest people do to overcome the Quran (Verse 26)?
questions.answer: They said, 'Do not listen to this Quran and make noise during it that you may overcome'.

questions.text: Who descends upon those who say 'Our Lord is Allah' and then remain steadfast?
questions.answer: The angels, giving them glad tidings of Paradise and telling them not to fear or grieve.

questions.text: Who is better in speech than one who invites to Allah?
questions.answer: No one is better than the one who invites to Allah, does righteousness, and says 'Indeed, I am of the Muslims'.

questions.text: How should a believer repel an evil deed (Verse 34)?
questions.answer: Repel it by that which is better, so that an enemy may become like a close friend.

questions.text: Who is granted the ability to repel evil with good?
questions.answer: Only those who are patient and those who possess a great portion of good fortune.

questions.text: What should one do if a whisper from Satan (Shaytan) touches them?
questions.answer: Seek refuge in Allah.

questions.text: Why are the sun and the moon mentioned in Verse 37?
questions.answer: As signs of Allah; believers are commanded not to prostrate to the sun or moon, but to the One who created them.

questions.text: How is the Quran described in Verse 41-42 regarding its protection?
questions.answer: As a mighty Book; falsehood cannot approach it from before it or from behind it.

questions.text: What would the disbelievers have said if the Quran was revealed in a foreign language?
questions.answer: They would have asked why its verses were not explained in detail and how a foreign book could be for an Arab messenger.

questions.text: Where will Allah show His signs to mankind so they know the truth (Verse 53)?
questions.answer: In the horizons (the universe) and within themselves.

questions.text: Is Allah sufficient as a witness over all things?
questions.answer: Yes, He is a witness over everything.
